summary: Addresses external review #9 feedback from greptile-apps[bot]: adds missing Replicas nil-default to DeploymentSpecDefaults and missing ServicePort.Protocol normalization to ServiceSpecDefaults. Both fixes prevent spurious update loops during reconciliation. Tests updated to cover nil-defaulting and explicit-value-preservation for each new default.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass for all changed code
Nil-defaulting case tested (Replicas defaults to 1, Protocol defaults to TCP)
Edge cases covered (empty ports list in ServiceSpecDefaults — handled by range loop)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing normalize package patterns (nil-guard then assign default)
Test structure matches existing table format with NewGomegaWithT(t)
Feature: CC-0005 tag present in test files

security_checklist.item: No user input handling — pure in-memory struct mutation

architecture_checklist.item: Changes are minimal and targeted to the review feedback
No scope creep beyond the two identified gaps
Normalizers correctly consumed by deployment package (deployment.go:50,52,112,114)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Nil checks are explicit guard clauses at top of function

defensive_checklist.item: Range loop over Ports handles empty slice correctly

verification.go_test: PASS — all 13 tests in normalize/ pass

review_steps.1_INTENT: Address two missing API-server default normalizations identified by greptile-apps[bot] that cause spurious update loops
review_steps.2_SCOPE: Changes strictly limited to 4 files in normalize/ — no scope creep
review_steps.3_VERIFICATION: go test ./normalize/ passes all 13 tests; go vet clean
review_steps.4_PATTERNS: Follows existing nil-guard-then-assign pattern used throughout normalize/ for RevisionHistoryLimit, ProgressDeadlineSeconds, Strategy, etc.
review_steps.5_TESTS: Both functions have defaulting test (nil input → expected default) and preservation test (explicit value → unchanged). Port Protocol test covers multiple ports.
review_steps.6_SECURITY: N/A — pure struct mutation functions with no I/O, no user input, no secrets
review_steps.7_COMPLEXITY: Minimal changes — 4-line Replicas block, 4-line Protocol loop. Simplest possible fix.
review_steps.8_COMPLETENESS: Both review comments fully addressed as suggested
